articles.title: How Project Maven taught the military to love AI
articles.summary: Project Maven, a military AI system using computer vision on drone footage, enabled the US military to accelerate targeting processes, striking over 1,000 targets in 24 hours during an Iran assault.

articles.title: AirPods, Touch Bars, and the rest of Tim Cook’s legacy
articles.summary: Tim Cook steps down as Apple CEO, with John Ternus as likely successor. The leadership change could bring significant shifts to Apple's product strategy and company direction.

articles.title: Musk vs. Altman is here, and it’s going to get messy
articles.summary: Elon Musk is suing OpenAI and Sam Altman, claiming fraud. A trial begins April 27th in Oakland, marking a legal battle between the cofounders over the company's direction.

articles.title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
articles.summary: Anthropic created a test marketplace where AI agents act as both buyers and sellers, conducting real transactions for goods using actual money.

articles.title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
articles.summary: Maine's governor vetoed a bill that would have imposed the nation's first statewide data center moratorium until November 2027, blocking restrictions on new data center construction.

articles.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
articles.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman apologized to Tumbler Ridge, Canada residents for failing to alert law enforcement about a mass shooting suspect.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway secured $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The platform has attracted 2 million developers by addressing limitations in legacy cloud infrastructure for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding capabilities, while Goose offers identical features free as open-source software running locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI tokens and coding challenges. The unconventional recruitment stunt successfully attracted thousands of talented engineers to the startup.

articles.title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
articles.summary: Prestigious university websites like Berkeley, Columbia, and Washington University are serving pornography and malware due to poor subdomain management by administrators. Hundreds of subdomains across 34+ universities have been exploited by scammers.

articles.title: In a first, a ransomware family is confirmed to be quantum-safe
articles.summary: Kyber ransomware is the first confirmed to use quantum-safe encryption, specifically the ML-KEM algorithm standardized by NIST, claiming protection against future quantum computer attacks.

articles.title: Microsoft issues emergency update for macOS and Linux ASP.NET threat
articles.summary: Microsoft released an emergency patch for ASP.NET Core to fix a high-severity vulnerability allowing unauthenticated attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges on Linux and macOS devices through faulty cryptographic signature verification.

articles.title: Three reasons why DeepSeek’s new model matters
articles.summary: DeepSeek released V4, its flagship model with improved long-context processing capabilities. The open-source model marks the company's most significant release since R1, which gained global recognition for strong performance despite limited computing resources.

articles.title: The Download: supercharged scams and studying AI healthcare
articles.summary: Cybercriminals are leveraging generative AI tools like ChatGPT for sophisticated attacks including phishing, deepfakes, and vulnerability scans, making cyberattacks faster and cheaper while overwhelming organizations' defenses.

articles.title: Health-care AI is here. We don’t know if it actually helps patients.
articles.summary: AI tools are increasingly used in hospitals for notetaking, patient record analysis, and medical imaging interpretation. However, despite demonstrating accuracy, there is limited evidence that these tools actually improve patient health outcomes.

articles.title: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use
articles.summary: DeepSeek-V4 introduces a million-token context window that enables AI agents to process and utilize vast amounts of information effectively, advancing practical AI application capabilities.

articles.title: How to Use Transformers.js in a Chrome Extension
articles.summary: Learn how to integrate Transformers.js, a machine learning library, into Chrome extensions to run AI models locally without server dependencies.

articles.title: Gemma 4 VLA Demo on Jetson Orin Nano Super
articles.summary: Google's Gemma 4 VLA (Vision Language Model) demonstrated running on NVIDIA's Jetson Orin Nano Super, showcasing efficient AI deployment on edge devices for multimodal tasks.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and innovation. The conversation explores upcoming trends and challenges in artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation. Examples include Hot Wheels using AI for innovative marketing and content management solutions.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft releases open-source toolkit for modern farming technology, enabling developers to build agricultural solutions using AI and data analytics.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Gemini provides AI-powered tips for organizing both physical and digital spaces, including cleaning schedules, inbox decluttering, and seasonal chore management.

articles.title: Here’s how our TPUs power increasingly demanding AI workloads.
articles.summary: Google's TPUs are specialized processors designed to handle increasingly demanding AI workloads efficiently. The company released a video explaining how these tensor processing units power advanced artificial intelligence applications.

articles.title: Elevating Austria: Google invests in its first data center in the Alps.
articles.summary: Google announces its first data center in Kronstorf, Austria, creating 100 direct jobs. The facility marks a significant infrastructure investment in the Alpine region.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo introduces a new method for resilient distributed AI training that improves fault tolerance and efficiency. The approach decouples communication between nodes, enabling faster training across large-scale systems.

articles.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with global consultancies to help organizations worldwide implement frontier AI technologies and accelerate their digital transformation.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS is a new audio model enabling precise control over AI speech generation through granular audio tags for expressive voice synthesis.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #238 discusses OpenAI's new GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows, Mistral's open-source Small 4 model family, and emerging research on Mamba 3 and attention residuals in AI architectures.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launches DLSS 5, a generative AI technology that enhances video game graphics in real-time. The technology boosts photorealism and has potential applications beyond gaming.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub code review pricing, and Cursor's trigger-based coding automations.

articles.title: Why AI agents need interaction infrastructure
articles.summary: Enterprises need interaction infrastructure to govern AI agents and prevent automation waste. Band, a new startup, raised $17M to build a dedicated layer enabling autonomous systems to coordinate across corporate networks.

articles.title: How AI models use real-time cryptocurrency data to interpret market behaviour
articles.summary: AI models leverage real-time cryptocurrency data streams to interpret dynamic market behavior, finding crypto's volatile and unpredictable patterns more challenging yet valuable for analysis compared to static datasets.

articles.title: The billion-dollar startup with a different idea for AI
articles.summary: Yann LeCun's AMI Labs secured $1 billion in funding to develop modular AI systems focused on specific use-cases, diverging from current large language model approaches and prioritizing long-term research over immediate products.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving debugging of collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses challenges in complex indoor environments by improving target localization, self-localization, and path planning.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to potential remote takeovers, representing a significant supply chain security risk.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for speed but weakens their ability to explore alternative solutions on complex problems, revealing a critical tradeoff in model efficiency.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As LLMs become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses surrounding them increasingly represent the true competitive advantage. Anthropic's Claude Code leak demonstrates that the engineering infrastructure matters more than base models.

articles.title: I've tested Sony headphones for years, and these tweaks get me the best audio - always
articles.summary: Sony headphones offer numerous software features that can optimize audio quality. Finding the right settings and tips tailored to your preferences significantly improves the listening experience.

articles.title: I'm not giving up on DJI cameras yet - not when they can upset my GoPro like this
articles.summary: The DJI Osmo Nano offers a modular, powerful alternative to GoPro for adventure recording. Its features make it a compelling option for content creators seeking flexibility.

articles.title: I saw the 'MacBook Pro for Linux users' for the first time, and it's a legit Windows threat
articles.summary: The Framework Laptop 13 Pro is a modular, premium ultraportable device positioned as an alternative to MacBook Pro for Linux users, potentially threatening Windows market dominance.

articles.title: Building AI Agents with Local Small Language Models
articles.summary: Building AI agents with local small language models democratizes AI development, enabling smaller companies and individuals to create agents previously requiring massive resources.

articles.title: Train, Serve, and Deploy a Scikit-learn Model with FastAPI
articles.summary: FastAPI is a popular framework for serving machine learning models due to its lightweight, fast, and user-friendly nature. It simplifies the process of training, deploying, and serving Scikit-learn models.

articles.title: AI Agent Memory Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Stateless AI agents lack memory of previous interactions, operating without context from past calls.

articles.title: Causal Inference Is Different in Business
articles.summary: Business causal inference differs from academic approaches due to decision-gravity factors. Understanding how decisions impact outcomes is critical in practical business contexts.

articles.title: The Essential Guide to Effectively Summarizing Massive Documents, Part 2
articles.summary: Part 2 explores extracting meaningful information from document clusters to unlock their potential for effective summarization of large documents.

articles.title: Introduction to Approximate Solution Methods for Reinforcement Learning
articles.summary: This article explores function approximation techniques in reinforcement learning and examines various approximation function choices for implementing RL algorithms effectively.

articles.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 powers Codex, an agentic coding application running on NVIDIA's GB200 infrastructure. Over 10,000 NVIDIA employees across departments are using it to achieve significant productivity gains.

articles.title: Tag, You’re It: GeForce NOW Levels Up Game Discovery With Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ Labels
articles.summary: GeForce NOW adds in-app labels for Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ games, improving game discovery. The upgrade helps gamers easily identify available titles and maximize their cloud gaming membership value.

articles.title: Making Sense of the Early Universe
articles.summary: Article discusses methods for understanding the early universe's formation and characteristics. Explores how scientists interpret cosmic data to comprehend the universe's origins and initial conditions.

articles.title: Building Workforce AI Agents with Visier and Amazon Quick
articles.summary: Visier and Amazon Quick integrate workforce intelligence with internal policies to help employees make faster, better-informed decisions by connecting disparate data sources across organizations.

articles.title: Amazon Quick for marketing: From scattered data to strategic action
articles.summary: Amazon Quick solves marketing's data fragmentation by connecting scattered systems and tools into a unified platform that learns user priorities, enabling faster campaign insights and decision-making.

articles.title: Applying multimodal biological foundation models across therapeutics and patient care
articles.summary: AI multimodal biological foundation models integrate fragmented healthcare data like genomics, medical images, and patient records to enable better diagnosis, treatment decisions, and personalized medicine at scale.

articles.title: Build with DeepSeek V4 Using NVIDIA Blackwell and GPU-Accelerated Endpoints
articles.summary: DeepSeek launched V4-Pro and V4-Flash models optimized for efficient million-token context inference on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. V4-Pro features 1.6T total parameters with 49B active parameters for high-performance AI deployment.

articles.title: Federated Learning Without the Refactoring Overhead Using NVIDIA FLARE
articles.summary: NVIDIA FLARE enables federated learning without requiring code refactoring, allowing organizations to train AI models across distributed data sources while maintaining privacy and data security.

articles.title: Winning a Kaggle Competition with Generative AI–Assisted Coding
articles.summary: Three LLM agents generated 600,000+ lines of code and ran 850 experiments to win a Kaggle competition, demonstrating how AI-assisted coding accelerates machine learning development and iteration.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations view social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these solutions openly with others.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background leveraged unique perspectives to develop innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough advances in cutting-edge fields.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling practical long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping for cleaner action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying feature interactions in Large Language Models at scale to improve interpretability and transparency. This work aims to make LLM decision-making processes more transparent for safer, more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents an information-driven approach to imaging system design that uses noisy measurements and noise models to optimize how well imaging systems distinguish between objects.
